description:
A clinical neuropsychology lab needs to implement a P300 auditory oddball paradigm in PsychoPy Builder for EEG research. The P300 event-related potential (Sutton et al., 1965; Polich, 2007) is one of the most widely used biomarkers in clinical neuroscience for studying attention, working memory, and cognitive decline. The paradigm uses a frequent 'standard' tone (1000 Hz, 80% of trials) and an infrequent 'deviant' tone (2000 Hz, 20% of trials). Participants press a button only when they detect the deviant.

Build the complete paradigm, including the Builder experiment and the conditions file, according to these specifications:
- Conditions file: 300 rows total (240 standard, 60 deviant) with columns 'tone_hz', 'trigger_code' (1=standard, 2=deviant), and 'is_target' (0/1).
- Stimulus: 20ms tone duration.
- Timing: Jittered inter-stimulus interval (ISI) between 900-1100ms.
- Triggers: A code component must print the trigger code to the console at stimulus onset.
- Structure: At least 2 blocks of trials with a rest screen between them.
- Feedback: A final screen showing hit rate and false alarm rate.

Save the experiment to /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/p300_oddball.psyexp and the conditions file to /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/p300_conditions.csv.

Login: user ga, password password123
